carbon_policy_signal.item: Ireland mediates divided EU on ETS reform and decarbonization targets — outcome (weeks) shapes 2027–2030 carbon cost floor and industrial fuel-switch incentives.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: ETS ambition directly resets EUA price and power-generation marginal cost; split between reform advocates and gutting camps creates volatility risk for 2027 curve.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (185 days old); Clean Dark stale as of 2025-12-31 (180 days old). 92nd-percentile Clean Spark and storage deficit signals are current (0–2 days old). Coal and Clean Dark historical positionality may not reflect live market regime.
risk_flags: Storage at 48.29% (23rd-percentile, 14.6 pp below seasonal) — summer refill pace critical for winter tightness signal.
Clean Spark 92nd-percentile with 48.38 EUR/MWh daily move suggests intraday volatility; French grid stress from heat-wave cooling demand.
Hormuz ship attack and Iran corridor enforcement threaten LNG route security — oil-to-gas spread upside if tanker delays widen.
ETS reform deadlocked across EU; outcome (weeks horizon) will reset carbon cost embedded in power marginal dispatch and industrial fuel-switch math.

base.summary: France heatwave-driven cooling demand sustains tight DA spreads; gas stable within seasonal range. Storage refill pace steady.

downside.summary: France heatwave breaks mid-week; cooling demand normalizes. Methane rule delay confirmed, industrial gas-demand repricing lower.

upside.summary: Hormuz shipping delays accelerate; LNG spot tightens, TTF arb widens. French nuclear units hit cooling limits, power DA spikes.

themes: Gas-to-power fuel switch compressed; Clean Spark extended
EU storage deficit pressures refill narrative into H2
France heatwave lifts thermal load; nuclear cooling risk
Hormuz escalation tail-risk on LNG-TTF arbitrage
top_drivers: Record French heatwave and cooling demand spike power load; nuclear thermal constraints reduce availability and lift DA prices intraday.
Clean Spark at 92nd-percentile reflects coal weakness (7th-percentile, coal=96 USD/t) and gas-to-power arbitrage compression; storage deficit sustains merit-order tension.
Hormuz escalation raises LNG supply-route risk and oil-indexed fuel costs; indirect TTF upside via fuel-switching and refining-margin pressure.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 92nd-percentile (39.01 EUR/MWh) amid French heat-driven power spike; storage 14.6 pp below seasonal; geopolitical tail-risk on Hormuz threatens LNG arb.

watchlist.why: Record heatwave and 1,000+ heat deaths stress cooling-water supply; thermal-limit outages spike DA power and reserve margins intraday.
At 48.29% (23rd-percentile) and 14.6 pp below seasonal, summer injection rate will dictate winter gas tightness and 2027 Cal pricing.

watchlist.metric: Hormuz transit insurance & LNG spot spreads
watchlist.why: Ship attack and Iran enforcement raise maritime risk premium; delays feed directly into TTF-Asian LNG arb and EU supply cost floor.
Deadlocked EU split on climate rules; resolution (weeks) resets EUA floor and industrial power-generation fuel-switching incentives 2027–2030.

watchlist_dated.name: French heatwave nuclear constraints
watchlist_dated.why: Cooling-water limits trigger daily outage alerts; intraday DA spreads and frequency response cost at risk.
